highlights.DefaultBuilderProvider.cs: 60.8% (unchanged from v2; new IsCatalogDescription helper match-true path uncovered by C# tests)
highlights.Catalog/ExampleRenderer.cs: 85.2% (no test asserts math.add ExampleSpec rendered output)

findings.issue: IsCatalogDescription helper (lines 653-664) has 4 distinct match shapes ('X', 'X?', 'X = default', '%var% X') and zero C# unit tests. Coverage report shows lines 659-663 (the match-true path) are never executed by any C# test — every C# call falls through the early-return at line 658. The match-true path is exercised exclusively by Tests/Modules/Builder/ValidateValid/BuilderValidateValid.test.goal.
findings.impact: A regression that flipped IsCatalogDescription to always-return-false (or broke any of the 4 shape cases) would leave the C# suite 100% green and surface only when someone happens to rebuild BuilderValidateValid.test.goal. Same false-green class as v1's BuildingGuard regression: code reviews + green C# suite both miss it.
findings.suggestion: Add 4 unit tests, one per match shape: bare typeName ('int'), nullable ('int?'), with default ('int = 1'), %var% prefix ('%var% string'). Plus 2 negative cases proving the typeName anchor: real value that doesn't start with type ('hello' vs 'string'), real value that LOOKS like description but mismatches type ('int = 5' for a string-typed slot — should NOT skip). Helper is private static; expose as internal-static or test through the Validate() public surface.

findings.issue: ExamplesForLlm() static methods on the 5 math actions declare ExampleSpec[] payloads but no C# test asserts the rendered output (after ExampleRenderer.Render()) contains the expected math.add + variable.set chain. Loop.test.goal is the only signal — and it's a non-deterministic LLM-integration test.
findings.impact: A regression in ExampleRenderer.Render() (e.g. dropping the second action in a chain, or breaking the %__data__% chaining) would silently corrupt the LLM prompt. The math examples render into something the LLM doesn't recognize, the LLM falls back to mapping 'set %x% = %x% + 1' to a single literal variable.set, and Loop.test.goal goes red on the next rebuild. The C# suite stays green throughout.
findings.suggestion: Add MathExamplesTests.cs asserting that math.add.ExamplesForLlm()[1] (the RHS form) renders through ExampleRenderer.Render(spec, modules) into a string containing 'math.add' AND 'variable.set' AND '%__data__%'. Repeat for the other 4 math actions. Cheap (~30 lines), catches renderer regressions deterministically without depending on a live LLM.

findings.issue: F4 cluster — 23 PLang test failures in Tests/ (plus 1 lowercase-relocated test) introduced by the v2 builder squash 50351d8b. Spans Signing (9 fails), Identity (2), UI (2), Event (3), Goal-call (1), ContextVars (1), Crypto (1), Test/Discover (1), ErrorTypes (1), App/SetupGoal (1), ConditionCompound (1), ForeachDictionary (1), ForeachCallsGoalPerItem (1).
findings.impact: Real production runtime regressions, not test problems. Several show systemic patterns: 'Contract mismatch' errors in Signing suggest a contract-metadata path is broken; 'Action timeout.after.after not found' is a module/action routing bug (extra .after segment); foreach producing Actual:1 or Actual:2 instead of 3 suggests iteration-counter or shared-state bugs; UI render swallowing %title% but rendering %author% suggests parameter-resolution divergence. Each cluster needs its own diagnosis.
findings.suggestion: Coder explicitly scoped this commit to F2+F3. Send back for F4 unless the project decides to defer F4 to a separate branch. If deferred, document the scope decision in the commit message of the next commit so the verdict can be downgraded.

findings.issue: Carryover from v1/v2 — F5 locale-format coverage gap. Format-side InvariantCulture fix is correct at 3 sites, but no test sets Thread.CurrentCulture to it-IT or de-DE and asserts numeric round-trip produces '3.14' not '3,14'.
findings.impact: A regression that flipped any of the 3 sites back to conv.ToString() (without InvariantCulture) would silently break the @known annotation round-trip on European locales. CI runners are typically en-US so the bug never surfaces in test runs.
findings.suggestion: Add Phase05_NonInvariantCulture_FormatStaysInvariant test: set Thread.CurrentCulture=it-IT temporarily, exercise FormatValue / FormatFormalValue / ExampleRenderer with a double value, assert output is '3.14' not '3,14', restore culture in finally.

findings.issue: Carryover from v1/v2/v3 — promoteGroups action is not referenced in any .goal file under Tests/, tests/, or os/. 0% coverage. XML-doc claims 'exercised by bootstrap cycle' but the bootstrap cycle does not invoke it. The PromoteGroupsImmutableStep ActionError code path is unreachable from any goal.
findings.impact: If the action regresses, no test catches it. If the action is dead, it should be deleted. Either way, current state is dishonest signal.
findings.suggestion: Either delete the action (if dead) or wire it into BuildGoal.goal explicitly. Document the choice in good_to_know.md.

findings.issue: Carryover from v2 — test comment claims it's a 'sanity guard that auto-wrap doesn't engage on already-list inputs' but the input List<string> is consumed by the list-conversion branch at line 126, not the auto-wrap at line 156. Deleting the auto-wrap branch (lines 156-168) would NOT fail this test. It still validates a real path (list-conversion) so it's not a false green — but it doesn't test what its comment claims.
findings.impact: Cosmetic. Future readers will misunderstand the test's coverage claim.
findings.suggestion: Rename to reflect the list-conversion branch it actually tests, and update comment. The auto-wrap branch is already covered by the 3 other Gap 3 tests.

summary: F2 (catalog skip via IsCatalogDescription) and F3 (math examples for LLM) close cleanly at integration level — BuilderValidateValid PASS, Loop.test.goal PASS, .pr inspection confirms math.add chain. F4 cluster (23 PLang reds across Signing/Identity/UI/Event/etc.) untouched per coder's explicit scope. Both new closures lack direct C# unit tests — match-true path of IsCatalogDescription and ExamplesForLlm rendered output are exercised only by integration tests; same false-green class as v1's BuildingGuard regression. Recommend back to coder for F4 + small unit-test follow-ups; or downgrade to approved-with-followups if F4 is officially deferred to a separate branch.
